rows.title: Soil Fertility

text_1.eng: Soil fertility is how healthy and rich the soil is for growing plants.

text_2.eng: It means the soil has enough nutrients, water, and good structure to help plants grow well.

rows.text_1: Soil fertility is how healthy and rich the soil is for growing plants.
rows.text_2: It means the soil has enough nutrients, water, and good structure to help plants grow well.

rows.title: Soil Erosion

text_1.eng: Soil erosion is the process where the top layer of soil is washed away by wind, water, or human activity.

text_2.eng: It makes the soil less fertile and harder for plants to grow.

rows.text_1: Soil erosion is the process where the top layer of soil is washed away by wind, water, or human activity.
rows.text_2: It makes the soil less fertile and harder for plants to grow.

rows.title: Difference between healthy and unhealthy soil

title.eng: Difference between healthy and unhealthy soil

text_1.eng: Take a sample of your farm’s best and worst soil. Compare:

text_2.eng:
* Texture (soft vs hard)
 * Plant growth (strong vs weak)
 * Presence of organic matter

rows.text_1: Take a sample of your farm’s best and worst soil. Compare:
rows.text_2:
* Texture (soft vs hard)
 * Plant growth (strong vs weak)
 * Presence of organic matter

rows.title: Unhealthy Soil

image_text.eng:
* Little or no organic matter
 * Hard or compacted
 * Dries out quickly
 * Crops grow weak or poorly

rows.image_text:
* Little or no organic matter
 * Hard or compacted
 * Dries out quickly
 * Crops grow weak or poorly

rows.title: Healthy Soil

image_text.eng:
* Contains compost (decomposed plant material)
 * Soft and easy to dig/work
 * Holds moisture well
 * Supports strong, healthy crops
 * Has micro-organisims like: termites, ants, worms, etc

rows.image_text:
* Contains compost (decomposed plant material)
 * Soft and easy to dig/work
 * Holds moisture well
 * Supports strong, healthy crops
 * Has micro-organisims like: termites, ants, worms, etc

text_1.eng: Here are some of the things that makes our soil loose fertility and their solutions

section_1_extra_text.eng:
Use organic manure like:
 * Garden compost
 * Farm yard manure
 * Bokashi
 * Green manure

section_2_extra_text.eng:
* Recycle feed animals.
 * Lay down for livestock to sleep so as to trap the animal waste like urine then use it as manure on your farm.

section_3_text.eng: Mono cropping- Planting one type of crop

section_3_extra_text.eng: Do crop rotation and ensure you check the nutients the crops are offering to the soil

rows.text_1: Here are some of the things that makes our soil loose fertility and their solutions
rows.section_1_extra_text:
Use organic manure like:
 * Garden compost
 * Farm yard manure
 * Bokashi
 * Green manure
rows.section_2_extra_text:
* Recycle feed animals.
 * Lay down for livestock to sleep so as to trap the animal waste like urine then use it as manure on your farm.
rows.section_3_text: Mono cropping- Planting one type of crop
rows.section_3_extra_text: Do crop rotation and ensure you check the nutients the crops are offering to the soil

rows.title: Agroecology

text_1.eng: Agroecology is just farming in a way that works with nature.

text_2.eng: It helps keep your soil healthy and your farm strong. Here’s how:

rows.text_1: Agroecology is just farming in a way that works with nature.
rows.text_2: It helps keep your soil healthy and your farm strong. Here’s how:

section_1_text.eng: Plant different crops and keep different varieties of animals.

section_1_extra_text.eng:
Some plants grow fast, some slow this way, if one fails, others still give you food and income. 
You can also harvest more than once in a season and have a balanced diet for your family.
helps with pest control too

rows.section_1_text: Plant different crops and keep different varieties of animals.
rows.section_1_extra_text:
Some plants grow fast, some slow this way, if one fails, others still give you food and income. 
You can also harvest more than once in a season and have a balanced diet for your family.
helps with pest control too

section_1_text.eng:
Use farm waste, like cow dung, to make compost or biogas—it can make your soil fertile and save money.
plants to eat people and livestock to manure

rows.section_1_text:
Use farm waste, like cow dung, to make compost or biogas—it can make your soil fertile and save money.
plants to eat people and livestock to manure

section_1_text.eng: Don’t plant the same crop in the same spot every year.

section_1_extra_text.eng: Some crops take a lot of nutrients from the soil, others give back. Rotating them keeps the soil rich and prevents it from getting tired.

rows.section_1_text: Don’t plant the same crop in the same spot every year.
rows.section_1_extra_text: Some crops take a lot of nutrients from the soil, others give back. Rotating them keeps the soil rich and prevents it from getting tired.

section_1_text.eng: Choose crops and animals that survive well in your area.

section_1_extra_text.eng: Some crops and animals can handle dry spells, others wet conditions. This way, your farm can recover from tough weather.

rows.section_1_text: Choose crops and animals that survive well in your area.
rows.section_1_extra_text: Some crops and animals can handle dry spells, others wet conditions. This way, your farm can recover from tough weather.

section_1_text.eng: Talk with other farmers, share your ideas and learn from theirs.

rows.section_1_text: Talk with other farmers, share your ideas and learn from theirs.

text_1.eng: These are organic manure that you can make at your home using basic materials that are easily found in your home and farm.

text_2.eng: Ensure to watch videos at the 'Practical Section' on how to make these manures and the ingredents used.

rows.text_1: These are organic manure that you can make at your home using basic materials that are easily found in your home and farm.
rows.text_2: Ensure to watch videos at the 'Practical Section' on how to make these manures and the ingredents used.

section_1_text.eng: Bokashi is a type of organic manure that’s faster to make than regular compost. It is partially fermented before use, and decomposition continues even after you apply it to the soil.

rows.section_1_text: Bokashi is a type of organic manure that’s faster to make than regular compost. It is partially fermented before use, and decomposition continues even after you apply it to the soil.

rows.title: Compost manure

text_1.eng:
There are two types of compost: **Boma compost and Garden compost.**
The time it takes for compost to be ready depends on the size of the pile, the materials used, and the moisture content—smaller piles generally take longer to decompose.

text_2.eng: The compost **always** be kept under shade to avoid direct sunlight that makes the compost loose its nutrients.

section_1_text.eng: Made by laying farm residues in a livestock shed to collect animal urine, which is rich in nitrogen. After some time, the mixture is collected and applied to your farm.

section_2_text.eng: Garden Compost is an organic fertilizer made by decomposing natural materials, primarily crop residues, until they turn into a rich, soil-like substance.

section_2_extra_text.eng: When collecting materials, the focus is on adding nutrients the soil needs, especially nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K).

rows.text_1:
There are two types of compost: **Boma compost and Garden compost.**
The time it takes for compost to be ready depends on the size of the pile, the materials used, and the moisture content—smaller piles generally take longer to decompose.
rows.text_2: The compost **always** be kept under shade to avoid direct sunlight that makes the compost loose its nutrients.
rows.section_1_text: Made by laying farm residues in a livestock shed to collect animal urine, which is rich in nitrogen. After some time, the mixture is collected and applied to your farm.
rows.section_2_text: Garden Compost is an organic fertilizer made by decomposing natural materials, primarily crop residues, until they turn into a rich, soil-like substance.
rows.section_2_extra_text: When collecting materials, the focus is on adding nutrients the soil needs, especially nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K).

section_1_text.eng: Involves planting leguminous crops such as beans, soya, or lablab, allowing them to grow until about 50% of flowering, then cutting them down before planting your main crop.

section_1_extra_text.eng: Lablab is the recommended choice as it serves as a cover crop; its seeds are expensive and highly nutritious.
